title: betula fontqueri subsp. parvibracteata
intro: Betula fontqueri subsp. parvibracteata, also known as the Canary Islands birch, is a subspecies of birch tree native to the Canary Islands. The medicinal properties of Betula fontqueri subsp. parvibracteata include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its various health benefits. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, improving wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity against certain bacteria and fungi. Herbal preparations of Betula fontqueri subsp. parvibracteata can be made into teas, tinctures, and infusions for medicinal use, but further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
